task: Generate a NOVEL method (distinct from the attached 1C-MIM paper) to identify a different skeletal-muscle-rejuvenating metabolite cocktail, and design the first pilot experiment.

source_paper.citation: Hernandez-Benitez et al., Cell Reports Medicine 5:101449 (2024)
source_paper.their_method: FORWARD: track transient metabolite waves during early cell differentiation across 3 progenitor systems -> intersect enriched pathways -> methionine/1-carbon + polyamine network -> hand-picked 1C-MIM (methionine, threonine, glycine, putrescine, SAM, cysteine)
source_paper.their_validated_mechanism: Acetylation + cell-cycle re-entry / regenerative competence (NOT global methylation-clock reversal, which they call 'minor')

my_method.name: CoRe — Cofactor-Reversal inversion of the muscle aging axis
my_method.principle: REVERSE direction: measure the muscle aging axis directly, then invert it through COFACTOR-LIMITED chromatin/metabolic enzymes to find FEEDABLE metabolites, selected by a combinatorial axis-coverage objective (not pathway hand-picking).
my_method.why_novel: Opposite starting point (aging state vs differentiation dynamics); mechanism-anchored enzyme layer vs pathway enrichment; combinatorial optimization vs manual selection; yields ZERO-overlap cocktail.

steps.title: Aging axis
steps.action: DESeq2 old-vs-young in Tabula Muris Senis bulk limb muscle (GSE132040), sex-adjusted

result.contrast: old {21,24,27 mo} vs young {3 mo}, sex-adjusted
result.validation: Canonical aged-muscle program: denervation/NMJ (Chrng +3.6, Sln +2.6), embryonic myosin (Myh3 +1.3), stress (Gadd45a, Runx1, Ampd3)

steps.title: 1C-MIM signature
steps.action: DESeq2 1C-MIM-vs-control in the paper's own GSE229533 aged quadriceps

result.markers_reproduced: Saa3 -2.8, Timp1 -2.8, Clu -0.8, Mt1 -1.9, Mt2 -2.3 (DOWN); Lep +1.4, Gdf5 +1.4 (UP) — all match the paper

steps.title: Premise test
steps.action: Correlate 1C-MIM effect with aging axis (global + per module)

result.verdict: 1C-MIM does NOT globally reverse the aging transcriptome (weakly POSITIVE corr).
result.module_finding: 1C-MIM reverses the age-ELEVATED oxidative-stress/metallothionein module (reversal +0.42) and drives a satellite/regeneration program (+0.34); it does not touch mito/senescence.
result.reconciliation: Consistent with the paper: DNA-methylation-clock reversal was 'minor'; mechanism is REGENERATIVE COMPETENCE (cell-cycle re-entry, satellite activation, acetylation), not global age reversal.
result.method_implication: The correct optimization target is NOT the global old-vs-young vector. It is the cofactor-limited epigenetic/metabolic ENZYME layer that gates regenerative competence and is dysregulated with age. This motivates the cofactor-reversal method.

steps.title: Cofactor map
steps.action: Map 6 chromatin/metabolic enzyme axes, each gated by a feedable cofactor; ground supply genes in Reactome

result.reactome_grounding: each axis maps to its canonical cofactor-metabolism pathway (Coenzyme A biosynthesis, Nicotinate metabolism, TCA cycle, Metabolism of polyamines, Riboflavin metabolism, Methylation)

result.positive_control: Method ranks Acetyl-CoA (0.70) ABOVE the SAM/methylation reference (0.68), independently recovering the paper's validated acetylation mechanism from an orthogonal direction.

steps.title: Select cocktail
steps.action: Submodular axis-coverage optimizer (maximize score, penalize inter-axis redundancy, lambda=1.2)

result.key_result: SAM/methionine (1C-MIM's defining axis) is NEVER selected even when allowed to compete — redundant/conflicting with aKG-demethylation (R=0.60) and acetyl-CoA (R=0.55).

steps.title: Pilot + power
steps.action: 4-arm CTX regeneration assay in aged mice; power from paper's effect sizes

result.power: 80% for CoRe-vs-vehicle at d=1.2 (paper's reported regeneration effect)
result.differentiator: cofactor + chromatin target-engagement readouts (NAD+, spermidine, aKG, 5hmC, H3ac)

final_cocktail.overlap_with_1CMIM: zero shared metabolites (putrescine->spermidine is precursor->product in the shared polyamine family)

_dose_note: Cocktail doses are representative starting values anchored to the cited primary studies (DOIs under references_confirmed), to confirm per protocol and titrate in the pilot; not lifted verbatim.
